Name: Flyway State Based Migration
Description:
Step template to leverage Flyway to deploy migration scripts.  This is the latest and greatest Flyway step template that leverages all the newest features of both Flyway and Octopus Deploy.

- You can include the flyway executables in your package, if you include the `flyway` (Linux) or `flyway.cmd` (Windows) in the root of the package this step template will automatically find them.
- You can use this with an execution container, negating the need to include Flyway in the package. If Flyway isn't found in the package it will attempt to find `/flyway/flyway` (when using Linux containers) or `flyway` in the environment path and use that.
- Support for Flyway State Based commands, including the `undo` command.
- Support for flyway community, teams, enterprise, and pro editions.  

Please note this requires Octopus Deploy **2019.10.0** or newer along with PowerShell Core installed on the machines running this step.
AWS EC2 IAM Authentication requires the AWS CLI to be installed.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Package.Value
Parameters.Label: Flyway Package
Parameters.HelpText:
**Required**

The package containing the migration scripts you want Flyway to run.  Please refer to [documentation](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/concepts/migrations) for core concepts and naming conventions.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Executable.Path
Parameters.Label: Flyway Executable Path
Parameters.HelpText:
**Optional**

The path of the flyway executable.  It can either be a relative path or an absolute path.

When not provided, this step template will test for the following.  The step template places precedence on the version of the flyway included in the package. If Flyway is NOT found in the package, it will attempt to see if it is installed on the server by checking common paths.

Running on `Linux`:
- `.flyway`: the package being deployed includes flyway and is running on Linux
- `/flyway/flyway`: The default path for the Linux execution container.

Running on Windows:
- `\.flyway.cmd`: the package being deployed includes flyway and is running on Windows
- `flyway`: the package is in the path on the Windows VM or Windows Execution container.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Command.Value
Parameters.Label: Flyway Command
Parameters.HelpText:
**Required**

The [flyway command](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/usage/commandline/) you wish to run.

- `Check`: Produces a report of changes from the `schema model` against the target `environment`
- `Deploy`: Executes the scripts generated to make the target database look like the model.
- `Prepare`: Compares the schema model against the target database and generates scripts to make the database conform to the model.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Email.Address
Parameters.Label: Email address
Parameters.HelpText: The email address associated with the Personal Access Token (PAT).

Parameters.Name: Flyway.PersonalAccessToken
Parameters.Label: Personal Access Token (PAT)
Parameters.HelpText:
The Personal Access Token (PAT) to authenticate with for Enterprise features.  

Replaces the License Key.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Target.Url
Parameters.Label: -Url
Parameters.HelpText:
**Required**

The [URL](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/configuration/parameters/url) parameter used in Flyway.  This is the URL of the database to run the migration scripts on in the format specified in the default flyway.conf file.

Examples:
- SQL Server: `jdbc:sqlserver://host:port;databaseName=database`
- Oracle: `jdbc:oracle:thin:@//host:port/service` or `jdbc:oracle:thin:@tns_entry`
- MySQL: `jdbc:mysql://host:port/database`
- PostgreSQL: `jdbc:postgresql://host:port/database`
- SQLite: `jdbc:sqlite:database`

Please refer to [documentation](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/database/sqlserver) for further examples.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Source.Schema.Model
Parameters.Label: Source Schema Model (optional).
Parameters.HelpText: Name of the source schema model used for the comparison operation.  Not all database technologies, such as MSSQL, use this option.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Target.Schema
Parameters.Label: Target Schema (optional)
Parameters.HelpText: Name of the target schema to deploy to.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Authentication.Method
Parameters.Label: Authentication Method
Parameters.HelpText: Method used to authenticate to the database server.

DisplaySettings.Octopus.SelectOptions:
awsiam|AWS EC2 IAM Role
azuremanagedidentity|Azure Managed Identity
gcpserviceaccount|GCP Service Account
usernamepassword|Username\Password
windowsauthentication|Windows Authentication

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Database.User
Parameters.Label: -User
Parameters.HelpText:
**Optional**

The [user](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/configuration/parameters/user) used to connect to the database.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Database.User.Password
Parameters.Label: -Password
Parameters.HelpText:
**Optional**

The [password](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/configuration/parameters/password) used to connect to the database.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Command.Schemas
Parameters.Label: -Schemas
Parameters.HelpText:
**Optional**

Comma-separated case-sensitive list of [schemas](https://flywaydb.org/documentation/configuration/parameters/schemas) managed by Flyway. 

Example: `schema1,schema2`

Flyway will attempt to create these schemas if they do not already exist and will clean them in the order of this list. If Flyway created them, then the schemas themselves will be dropped when cleaning.

The first schema in the list will act as the default schema.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Additional.Arguments
Parameters.Label: Additional arguments
Parameters.HelpText: Any additional arguments that need to be passed (ie `-table="MyTable")

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Transaction
Parameters.Label: Execute in transaction?
Parameters.HelpText: Tick this box if you want the deployment to execute within a transaction.

Parameters.Name: Flyway.Generate.Undo
Parameters.Label: Generate Undo Script?
Parameters.HelpText: Tick this box if you want Flwyay to generate an `Undo` script.  `Undo` scripts will be created as an output variable for `Prepare` and attached as an Octopus Artifact for `Deploy`.
